other_observers: None; I am original finder and only observer that I know about.
specific_location: On west shore of Navajo Reservoir about two hundred yards north of marina
description: Medium sized warbler with sturdy bill and tail cocked slightly up. Upper parts mostly a bright yellow green with very faint stripes on back. Distinct white eye ring and bill fairly heavy and lighter colored near base particularly on the lower mandible. Sides of face were a light gray which extended down sides of neck to flanks just below wings. Chin and throat were clean white which extended to belly, vent and undertail coverts. There were faint tones of yellow at the forward vent area. Wings were blackish to gray with two bold white wing bars. Upper tail was gray and whitish underneath. Legs were dark. Wings drooped slightly.Behaviors: Found with a mixed flock of yellow-rumped warblers, sparrows, and kinglets. Actively foraging for food. Bright yellow green back was immediately visible and identified this bird as different from common area birds. Tail was frequently cocked slightly up and wings were also slightly drooped.Call: not heardPlumage: first fall
photographer: Photographer: Jim Beatty, Jim Beatty, Jim Beatty
